name: Color
summary: The Color class is used to create and manipulate colors within SketchUp Models. The class can also be used the same way with LayOut documents. For methods that accept a Color object, such as the face.material method, you can pass in an actual Color object, or an object that can be converted to a Color. For example: SketchUp ships with several built in colors in the Materials Browser. These colors are listed in the following table. constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The new method is used to create a new Color object.
constructors.remarks: The new method is used to create a new Color object.

params.name: alpha
params.default: 255) => Sketchup::Color | initialize(red, green, blue, alpha = 1.0) => Sketchup::Color | initialize(name) => Sketchup::Color | initialize(hex

methods.name: names
methods.summary: The names method is used to retrieve an array of all color names recognized by SketchUp.
methods.remarks: The names method is used to retrieve an array of all color names recognized by SketchUp. In general, whenever a method wants a color, you can pass in a String with one of these names.

methods.name: blend
methods.summary: The #blend method is used to blend two colors.
methods.remarks: The #blend method is used to blend two colors. The blended color will be the result of taking (1 - weight) * color1 + weight * color2. If weight = 0, you will get color2. If weight = 1 you will get color1.

params.name: color2
params.doc: The second color to be blended (with this color).

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The #to_a method is used to convert a Color object to an Array object.
methods.remarks: The #to_a method is used to convert a Color object to an Array object. The returned array will contain 4 integer values (RGBA) between 0 and 255.

methods.name: to_i
methods.summary: The #to_i method is used to convert a Color object to an 32 bit integer.
methods.remarks: The #to_i method is used to convert a Color object to an 32 bit integer.

methods.name: to_s
methods.summary: The #to_s method returns a string representation of the Sketchup::Color object, in the form of “Color(255, 255, 255, 255)”.
methods.remarks: The #to_s method returns a string representation of the Sketchup::Color object, in the form of “Color(255, 255, 255, 255)”.

properties.summary: The #== method checks to see if the two Sketchup::Colors are equal.
properties.remarks: The #== method checks to see if the two Sketchup::Colors are equal. This checks whether the RGBA values are the same. In versions prior to SketchUp 2018 two color objects with the same values would be considered different.

properties.name: alpha
properties.summary: The #alpha method is used to retrieve the opacity of the color.
properties.remarks: The #alpha method is used to retrieve the opacity of the color. A value of 0 is transparent, 255 is opaque.

properties.name: blue
properties.summary: The #blue method is used to retrieve the blue value of a color.
properties.remarks: The #blue method is used to retrieve the blue value of a color. Value range is 0 to 255.

properties.name: green
properties.summary: The #green method is used to retrieve the green value of a color.
properties.remarks: The #green method is used to retrieve the green value of a color. Value range is 0 to 255.

properties.name: red
properties.summary: The #red method is used to retrieve the red component of a RGB Color.
properties.remarks: The #red method is used to retrieve the red component of a RGB Color. Value range is 0 to 255.
